title: Edge Case Hardening & Documentation
goal: Harden the application against edge cases identified in the PRD (empty responses, network timeouts, rate limits, missing fields), and update the README with complete setup instructions, usage guide, and architectural overview.

in_scope: Add timeout handling for inference calls (default 60 seconds per call)
Handle empty or whitespace-only prompts — show warning and prevent submission
Handle empty model responses (output_text is empty string) — display placeholder text
Handle rate limiting (HTTP 429) with a user-friendly error message per column
Handle network connectivity errors with descriptive messages
Validate that deployment discovery timeout is handled gracefully
Ensure all user-facing error messages are actionable and non-technical
Update README.md with: project description, prerequisites, setup instructions, usage guide, folder structure, pricing config notes, known limitations
out_of_scope: Retry logic or automatic recovery from transient errors
Automated test suite creation (tests are out of planning scope)
Performance optimization or caching beyond what exists

inputs: PRD section 5.3 — Advanced features and edge cases
outputs: Updated src/inference.py — with timeout and enhanced error handling
Updated src/app.py — with input validation and edge case UX
dependencies: Parts 01–08 — All modules must be implemented and functional
checklist: Add a timeout parameter to run_inference() (default: 60 seconds) and pass it to the SDK call
Catch timeout exceptions specifically and return a descriptive error: 'Request timed out after 60 seconds'
Catch HTTP 429 (rate limit) responses and return error: 'Rate limit exceeded. Please wait and try again.'
In app.py, validate that the prompt is not empty or whitespace-only before allowing submission
Display st.warning() if the user clicks Submit with an empty prompt
In results display, handle empty output_text — show 'Model returned an empty response' placeholder
Add a timeout to the deployment discovery call to prevent indefinite hangs
Ensure all error messages avoid exposing internal stack traces or sensitive data
Update README.md: add project title and description section
Update README.md: add prerequisites (Python version, Azure AI Foundry resource)
Update README.md: add installation section (pip install, env var setup)
Update README.md: add usage section (how to launch, workflow steps)
Update README.md: add pricing configuration section (how to update model_pricing.json)
Update README.md: add known limitations section (single resource, static pricing, no persistence)
acceptance_criteria: Inference calls respect the timeout and return a clean error on timeout
Rate limit (429) errors produce user-friendly messages, not raw HTTP errors
Empty prompts are rejected with a clear warning before submission
Empty model responses show a descriptive placeholder instead of blank space
Network errors during discovery show actionable messages in the UI
README.md contains all sections needed for a new user to set up and use the tool
No stack traces or internal errors are visible to the end user under any tested scenario
The app remains stable and usable after encountering any single edge case
review_focus: All error messages are actionable — the user knows what to do next
No sensitive data (API key, internal paths) appears in error messages
README instructions are accurate and match the actual project structure
Timeout values are reasonable (not too short to cause false failures)
Edge case handling does not introduce regression in the happy path
risks_or_notes: SDK-specific timeout configuration may differ from a simple parameter — verify SDK docs
Rate limiting behavior may vary by model deployment and Azure subscription tier
README content should be validated by actually following the setup steps
Some edge cases may only be testable against a live Azure resource
definition_of_done: All identified edge cases are handled with user-friendly error messages
Timeout handling works for both inference and discovery calls
Empty prompt validation prevents accidental submission
README.md is complete and a new user can set up the project by following it
The app doesn't crash under any single-point-of-failure scenario
Manual testing confirms the full happy-path and error-path workflows
